description: Create a content slide titled 'Questions leaders should ask' with a two-column layout: a visual/image panel on the left and seven role-based bullet lists on the right.

left.body: Illustrative image of a climber on a rock formation.

items.sub:
How confident am I that our organization could respond in real time to the next major shock?
Do we have protocols in place to maintain stakeholder trust amid a significant crisis?

For each material risk, do we have clear signals, thresholds, owners and preauthorized actions?
How should we rethink our approach to governance?

Which operational risks escalate in hours or days – and which of those are still reviewed quarterly?
After every crisis or near miss, do we systematically refine signals, reset thresholds and update protocols?

Are our materiality thresholds tied to trailing EBITDA, or to our ability to achieve strategic objectives – and how does that change capital allocation and risk appetite?
Do we have trigger-based financial responses defined before a crisis strikes?

Does our current use of technology in risk management support real time visibility, or reinforce periodic oversight?
What enterprise data must exist so our highest-risk signals are measurable, owned and refreshed at the right cadence – and where are we blind?

Is our workplace culture designed for speed with accountability – or safety through delay?
Which roles need stronger risk fluency, and which need curious and contrarian mindsets – and how will we hire or develop for that?

Which legal risks can escalate in days – and do we have protocols to respond in real time?
Do we understand how moving at speed could raise new legal risks or liabilities – and how to mitigate such vulnerabilities?

footer.source: How can reimagining risk prepare you for an unpredictable world? | Global Risk Transformation Series
